message: Trays cooled overnight. Heat distribution was inconsistent - some bites charred, others pale. We're redoing the ratio.
The uneven browning is actually the story - shows us which bites hit the heat hardest, which ones stayed protected in the middle of the tray.
That's not a story, that's a failure. We need consistency or the whole batch looks amateur.
You're right - consistency reads as care, and we need readers to trust what they're biting into. What if we test a lower rack position first?
Margaret nailed it though - if half the batch looks burnt, nobody's thinking "oh how artisanal," they're thinking we don't know what we're doing.
The middle rack sits too close to the element. Move everything down, drop the temp by two percent, pull samples at eight minutes instead of waiting for the full bake.
